Ail..Mind: A continued dreamy state of mind though awake. Child cries all the time. Sees little animals like rats running around. Feels a rat or something small crawling up the limb and over the body. There seems to be constant loss of memory, even the things spoken of a moment ago go right out of the mind. Constant forgetfulness. All past events are forgotten. Past events are forgotten or remembered as belonging to someone else, or as matters read.
Ail..Mouth: From the mouth and nose come foetid odors.
Ail..Skin: "Irregular, patchy, livid eruption, disappearing on pressure and returning very slowly; interspersed with small vesicles, worse on forehead, head, neck and chest."
Ail..Clinical: Dr. Wells used this remedy in a number of cases, as it was at that time an epidemic remedy for scarlet fever, in Brooklyn, and many patients were saved by it. It seemed to be able to change the character of the malignant forms of scarlet fever into a mild type.
Ail..Face: "Face dark as mahogany." Purple, bloated and puffed, besotted face.
Ail..Throat: "Throat much swollen, dark red, almost purple in color. Diphtheria with extreme prostration. Throat livid, swollen; tonsils prominent and studded with deep ulcers."
Ail..Extremities, pain: "During chill hunger, empty feeling, intolerable pain in back of neck, upper part of back and hip joint."
Ail..Face, expression: "Great prostration and a countenance indicating much distress."
Ail..Throat, discoloration: "Throat much swollen, dark red, almost purple in color." "Throat livid, swollen; tonsils prominent and studded with deep ulcers."
Ail..Fever: "Chill at 8 A.M. with chill, heat and sweat." During the chill vomiting of food and piercing pain over the hip. Chill is preceded by malignant eruption, especially on the face and forehead.
Ail..Nose: "Pupils widely dilated; copious, thin, ichorous and bloody, discharge from the nose." "Nostrils congested."
Ail..Skin, eruptions: "Eruption plentiful, of a bluish tint. Typhoid scarlatina." "Eruption is slow to make its appearance, remains livid." "Body and limbs covered with an irregular patchy eruption of a very livid color."
Ail..Throat, ulcers: "Throat livid, swollen; tonsils prominent and studded with deep ulcers."
Ail..Clinical, ulcers: as soon as the blisters break ulceration will take place if the child live long enough; but these are the deadly cases, the malignant type.
Ail..Extremities, pain, burning: "Burning in the palms and soles, hunts to find a cool place to put them."
Ail..Face, expression, anxious: There is in the earlier stages of this zymotic state great anxiety and restlessness. "Great prostration and a countenance indicating much distress."
Ail..Nose, coryza: "Pupils widely dilated; copious, thin, ichorous and bloody, discharge from the nose." "Nostrils congested."
